title: Living Doc Compositor Development Overview
subtitle: A working view of the tool as runtime, schema language, template system, and AI-ready reasoning substrate.
scope: Development of the living-doc compositor across runtime UX, renderer behavior, convergence language, templates, and operational tooling.
sourceCoverage: Repo-local code, concept docs, template files, registry state, recent git history, and current GitHub issues inspected through gh CLI on 2026-04-15.
objective: Make the tool legible as one evolving system instead of a pile of UI work, renderer changes, schema decisions, and concept notes.
successCondition: A contributor can see the active development lanes, the model constraints that must not drift, the tools used to evolve the repo, and the ground-truth operating decisions that shape new work.

objectiveFacets.name: Compositor runtime
objectiveFacets.description: Interactive authoring surface — the compositor as a runtime embedded in every rendered doc.

objectiveFacets.name: Convergence language
objectiveFacets.description: Registry as the single source of truth for type vocabulary and structural contracts.

objectiveFacets.name: Renderer and snapshots
objectiveFacets.description: Portable HTML output and the canonical/portable distinction.

objectiveFacets.name: Templates and prompts
objectiveFacets.description: Template semantics as a workflow harness and registry-owned prompt guidance.

objectiveFacets.name: Library and sharing
objectiveFacets.description: Local manifest discovery, filtered sharing, and privacy boundaries.

objectiveFacets.name: Formal model integrity
objectiveFacets.description: Contracts that must not drift as the tool grows.

objectiveFacets.name: Operating decisions
objectiveFacets.description: Ground-truth choices that govern how new work is shaped.

invariants.name: Development lanes declare where the work lives
invariants.statement: Every Development Lanes card declares at least one codePaths entry so readers can jump from status to the files that carry it.

invariants.name: Formal model claims declare their authorities
invariants.statement: Every Core Model Constraints card declares primitiveRefs, invariantRefs, and authorityRefs so drift is traceable to an authoritative source.

invariants.name: Tooling cards explain how to use themselves
invariants.statement: Every Operational Tools card declares purpose, whenToUse, and invocation so the instrument layer is self-describing.

invariants.name: Registry owns type language
invariants.statement: New convergence types belong in scripts/living-doc-registry.json, not in renderer branches or compositor-only logic.

invariants.name: JSON owns canonical state
invariants.statement: Canonical JSON is the editable source; rendered HTML is the portable snapshot. Edits flow JSON → HTML, never the reverse.

callouts.title: How to use this doc
items: Use the capability and tooling sections to orient current work before opening individual files.
Use the formal-model and decision sections to avoid weakening the schema and snapshot contract while the tool grows quickly.

sections.title: Objective Coherence
sections.rationale: Projects the doc-root objective facets, coverage, and invariants as a derived view so coverage and governance stay visible alongside the work.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Current pressure pattern
items: Growth is happening in two directions at once: wider product/runtime surface and deeper formal semantics.
The active issue set clusters around left-rail UX, prompt-driven skill creation, snapshot lineage, renderer quality, stronger convergence typing, and the new library/sharing epic.

sections.rationale: Top-level counts and the current-pressure callout compress five active lanes into one glance; itemized work belongs in the sections below, not here.

sections.title: Development Lanes
sections.rationale: Five parallel work lanes with independent status, code paths, and tickets — readers scan implementation state side by side rather than reading one narrative.

data.name: Embedded compositor runtime and editor surface
notes: The compositor is now the full runtime that must ship inside every rendered HTML, not a reduced viewer.
Current pressure is in the two-layer rail and drawer architecture plus the prompt sidecar that turns the current living doc into agent-ready context.
The hamburger menu now has an Advanced section with an in-compositor registry overview rendered from the embedded registry.
A settings panel is being introduced as the control surface for local library manifests and future share defaults.

data.name: Registry and convergence-language evolution
notes: The type language is expanding as migrated and newly created docs reveal stronger recurring semantic patterns.
The critical quality bar is that convergence types remain inference-time contracts; section-level presentation overrides have been removed so registry definitions own structural shape.
Every convergence type now carries promptGuidance so sparse new docs can still generate type-specific skill prompts without compositor fallback inference.

data.name: Renderer quality and portable snapshot output
notes: The renderer must preserve source fidelity while still producing standalone HTML that feels intentional enough to share.
Identity and lineage are now part of the output contract, but the visual-density and content-fit work is still an active lane.

data.name: Templates and reusable skill-generation surface
notes: Templates are no longer just starter files; they are becoming a stronger semantic harness for reusable workflows and agent prompts.
The prompt layer is moving away from selector-heavy packaging and toward document-derived skill identity built from registry, payload, template semantics, and registry-owned prompt guidance.

data.name: Local library and filtered sharing model
notes: The private `.gtd` catalog, in-tool library manifest, and shared-library manifest are now being treated as separate layers.
The epic splits the remaining work into settings observability, library filtering, filtered-index sharing, selected snapshot or bundle sharing, and privacy documentation.
Settings observability now reports manifest load state, sibling-discovery state, entry counts, editability, index-only entries, last-check time, and the local manifest refresh command.
The library drawer now supports text search, domain filtering, repo filtering, editable-only filtering, and per-entry source/editability badges.
HTML export now supports the first explicit sharing mode: a sanitized `shared-library-index-data` block plus a rendered `shared-library-index` section from the current library filters.
Selected portable snapshots are still intentionally deferred to the separate bundle/snapshot design ticket.

sections.title: Core Model Constraints
sections.rationale: These are formal model claims that must not drift as the tool grows, so primitives, invariants, authorities, and integrity status each need their own field.

data.name: Convergence type defines semantic and structural contract
data.definition: A convergence type is a semantic claim about what kind of convergence is happening, and it should determine the expected structural shape.
data.authorityScope: Registry definitions plus renderer and compositor behavior for all living docs.
notes: Section-level projection and column overrides were removed from the compositor UI, prompt payload, browser export, and Node renderer.
This matters because the repo is building living docs as an AI reasoning substrate, not just a page renderer.

data.name: Canonical JSON and portable HTML stay distinct
data.definition: Canonical JSON owns the editable document state; rendered HTML is the portable artifact used for sharing and local reasoning.
data.authorityScope: Rendering, sharing, and rerender workflows inside this repo and across sibling repos.
notes: Visible identity and lineage metadata exist to make drift legible rather than pretending portability does not create forks.

data.name: The registry is the single source of truth for type language
data.definition: New convergence types belong in the registry instead of being smuggled into renderer branches or compositor-only logic.
data.authorityScope: All templates, docs, renderer behavior, and compositor affordances in this repo.
notes: This is the contract that keeps future doc creation data-driven instead of ad hoc.

data.name: Prompt guidance is part of each convergence type contract
data.definition: Every convergence type carries promptGuidance with operatingThesis, keepDistinct, inspect, update, and avoid so prompt generation can render type-specific instructions directly from the registry.
data.authorityScope: Skill-prompt generation for template-backed and non-template living docs, especially sparse new documents where payload examples are thin.
notes: This makes Capability Surface, Operation, Formal Model, manuscript types, and other selected types materially change prompt behavior even before a template is involved.
The compositor should surface missing promptGuidance as a registry contract problem instead of silently synthesizing older fallback wording.

data.name: Template semantics shape workflow identity without overriding schema
data.definition: Template semantics can shape trigger conditions, workflow order, and skill identity while the registry slice and current payload remain authoritative for the specific document instance.
data.authorityScope: Prompt generation and reusable skill creation for template-backed living docs.
notes: This is the deeper semantic move that turns templates into workflow harnesses instead of just sample files.

sections.title: Operational Tools
sections.rationale: The section documents the instruments used to operate on the domain rather than the domain itself — purpose, when-to-use, and invocation are first-class per card.

data.name: Universal renderer
data.purpose: Render canonical JSON into a standalone HTML snapshot with the full compositor embedded.
data.whenToUse: Use whenever a living doc needs to be refreshed, shared, or moved into another reasoning context.
notes: This is the main bridge between editable JSON and portable artifact.
caveats: Rendered HTML can drift from canonical JSON, so rerender discipline matters.

data.name: Embedded compositor sync
data.purpose: Keep the checked-in rendered docs aligned with the current compositor runtime embedded inside them.
data.whenToUse: Use after editing `docs/living-doc-compositor.html` and before assuming the shipped examples match current behavior.
notes: This catches drift inside the repo's own examples before it leaks into external rerenders.
caveats: Syncing local examples does not refresh cross-repo living docs; those still need explicit rerenders from their source JSON.

data.name: Registry overview generator
data.purpose: Expose the current convergence-language surface so contributors can decide whether new work needs a new type or just a new document instance.
data.whenToUse: Use when repo changes start feeling like hidden schema work rather than just GUI or renderer work.
caveats: The overview reflects the registry as written; it does not validate whether sibling repos are already drifting from that language.

data.name: Embedded registry overview
data.purpose: Render all convergence types from the embedded registry inside the compositor's hamburger menu.
data.whenToUse: Use when a contributor needs to inspect the type language without leaving the compositor or loading a separate registry overview file.
notes: The modal shows grouped type cards with projection, structural contract, sources, status fields, and prompt guidance.
caveats: This is an inspection surface; editing convergence types still belongs in `scripts/living-doc-registry.json`.

data.name: Living-doc library manifest exporter
data.purpose: Project the private `~/.gtd/living-docs.json` catalog into a compositor-readable library manifest.
data.whenToUse: Use when the compositor should act as a cross-repo living-doc library instead of only discovering sibling JSON files.
notes: The default output is local and gitignored so raw private catalog details do not get committed accidentally.
The script also has a `--share-safe` mode for producing a filtered manifest shape that omits local filesystem paths.
caveats: Cross-repo entries may be index-only inside the browser unless their JSON is made available through a reachable manifest URL.

data.name: Compositor settings panel
data.purpose: Expose configuration for loading a sanitized library manifest and preparing share-library defaults.
data.whenToUse: Use from Menu -> Advanced -> Settings when changing how the compositor discovers the local living-doc library.
notes: Settings are stored separately from document autosave state in localStorage.
The library loader now tries the configured manifest first and can fall back to sibling JSON discovery.
The settings modal and library drawer now expose manifest and sibling-discovery load state, entry counts, source badges, editable/index-only badges, and last-check time.
When the default sharing mode is `Filtered library index`, HTML export embeds a separate sanitized `shared-library-index-data` JSON block and a rendered `shared-library-index` shared-context section.
caveats: Selected portable snapshots remain a planned design pass; the current export mode shares index metadata only.

data.name: Repo agent instructions
data.purpose: Keep contributors and code agents aligned on rendering rules, registry authority, and cross-repo living-doc discovery.
data.whenToUse: Use before GUI changes, rerenders, or cross-repo living-doc maintenance work.
data.invocation: Open `AGENTS.md` before changing compositor behavior or rerendering docs outside this repo.
notes: The current instructions also point agents at the external living-doc catalog when the source document lives in another repo.
caveats: The catalog itself lives outside this repo in `~/.gtd/living-docs.json`, so local repo links cannot fully encode that discovery layer.

sections.title: Ground-Truth Decisions
sections.rationale: Authoritative operating choices kept compact and explicit, deliberately separate from implementation work and from formal-model claims so they remain settled, not re-argued.

data.name: Rendered living docs must embed the complete compositor
notes: The shared artifact is the document plus the tool, not a viewer that strips authoring power.
Any rerendered HTML should carry the complete runtime even when opened from another repo or shared as a snapshot.

data.name: Type knowledge belongs in the registry, not in per-doc special cases
notes: When a recurring pattern appears, the answer is to promote it into an explicit convergence type rather than letting local rendering mutations carry semantic meaning.
This keeps the type system strong enough for both human and model reasoning.

data.name: Prompt UI should stay simple while prompt logic gets smarter
notes: The prompt panel should expose one useful prompt rather than a selector matrix.
Higher-order behavior belongs in prompt-shaping logic derived from registry, payload, template semantics, and repo references.

data.name: Cross-repo living-doc discovery is catalog work, not registry work
notes: The registry defines the living-doc language, but the actual cross-repo document inventory is maintained in `~/.gtd/living-docs.json`.
That separation matters because document location and sync workflow are operational metadata, not convergence-type schema.

data.name: Prompt steering belongs in registry promptGuidance, not fallback code
notes: The prompt panel should use convergence type promptGuidance directly for operating thesis, distinctions, inspection behavior, update behavior, and drift boundaries.
New convergence types are incomplete until they define promptGuidance; preserving that contract is more important than compatibility with older registry shapes.

data.name: Shared libraries must be explicit filtered manifests
notes: A shared living doc may carry surrounding library context, but only through a user-selected manifest subset.
The raw `.gtd` catalog remains private local control-plane data and should not be embedded into shared HTML by default.
Index-only sharing is the first safe mode; selected portable snapshots or bundles need an explicit second design pass.
